summary:
ACLU Complaint
Facts:
Plaintiff is an employee of Anchorage School District and is enrolled in the district's self funded PPO plan administered by AETNA ID: W1212-99544 GRP: 658742-31-013
Plaintiff suffers from Gender Dysphoria and requires gender affirming surgery as a medical necessity, this medical nesescity has been confirmed by 2 Psychologist and 1 Primary Care Physician.
The plaintiff has both gender affirming vaginoplasty and gender affirming breast augmentation surgery scheduled for 6/14/2023 and plaintiff's surgeon has requested preauthorization for the surgery.
AETNA as the administrator of the self-funded plan by the Anchorage School District has denied both required surgeries as having specific exclusions in the plan.
The Anchorage School District in Anchorage Alaska has a specific exclusion in place for "sexual enhancement" in their self funded health plan policy. This exclusion specifically excludes.  This is the exclusion used to deny me gender affirming surgery.
Any treatment, prescription drug, or supply to treat sexual dysfunction, enhance sexual performance or increase sexual desire, including: Surgery, prescription drugs, implants, devices or preparations to correct or enhance erectile function, enhance sensitivity or alter the shape of a sex organ Sex therapy, sex counseling, marriage counseling, or other counseling or advisory services..." 
The plaintiff has contacted the Benefits department of Anchorage School District with a request to remove or waive the blanket exclusion of gender affirming surgery.
The plaintiff has appealed with AETNA both denial

summary:
Daniel Dennett, “Where Am I?”
This article is an extended thought-experiment, meant to help the reader think philosophically about issues involving personal identity and survival. Here’s the basic idea.
Dennett describes an important job for which he was recruited by the Pentagon. They want him to go underground to retrieve a missile that got lodged under Tulsa, Oklahoma, and because the missile is extremely radioactive, they want him to leave his brain behind. (This is a fictional story, by the way.) He agrees and undergoes an operation to have his brain removed, placed in a vat, and connected via radio links to his body. He wakes up, goes to visit his brain floating in the vat, and he asks, “Where am I?”
It’s a tough question, given that his brain is in the vat at which he is now looking and his body is standing outside the vat looking in at his brain. He begins to think about the question philosophically, and gives names to his brain and his body to keep things straight. He names his bodiless brain “Yorick” and his brainless body “Hamlet.” He knows where Yorick is—in the vat. And he knows where Hamlet is—outside the vat. But the question is, Where’s Dennett? He runs through three possibilities: (1) Where Hamlet goes, there goes Dennett; (2) Where Yorick goes, there goes Dennett; and (3) Dennett is wherever he thinks he is. The problem is that each of these choices seems wrong.
Against option 1, Dennett appeals to the fact that it seems like he would be able to survive a simple brain transplant operation, in which his brain is placed in a different body and his old body i

summary:
Instructions for using the Gender Affirming Surgery Appeal Template
Dear community member,
Thank you for using our online template to complete your health insurance appeal. To learn more about the health insurance appeal process, please visit our website at https://transhealthproject.org/
Please read these instructions before attempting to use the template:
Text highlighted in yellow should be replaced by information relevant to your own particular situation.
We have also included instructions & descriptions of the type of information you should be putting in each section, highlighted in green
Please check that all text highlighted in yellow & green has been replaced/deleted before you send the appeal.
You are free to add, edit, rearrange, or delete any portion of the appeal as you see fit.
For the insurance company information header, please look at either your denial letter or your plan policy’s section on appealing denials. This is usually at the end of the policy. It should include an address and the name of the administrator that the appeal should be addressed to.
If the denial letter and your plan policy give you different addresses, or you cannot find this information, please call your health plan or your HR department if you get your health insurance from your employer.
Information for the member section should be found in your denial letter. You can get this information from your medical provider if you cannot find it.
** Please note that the use of this template does not create an attorney – client relationship between TLDEF and you. **

Sent via Fax/E-Mail/Ma

summary:
IN THE UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT
FOR THE DISTRICT OF CONNECTICUT
INTRODUCTION
Plaintiffs Binah Gordon, Kay Mayers, and Shay Natividad (“Plaintiffs”) bring this putative class action civil rights lawsuit on behalf of themselves and all similarly situated individuals against Defendant Aetna Life Insurance Company (“Aetna”), for denying them health insurance coverage for medically necessary gender affirming facial reconstruction surgeries and procedures (collectively, “GAFR”) under Aetna’s categorical coverage exclusion on such treatments, in violation of the prohibition on discrimination on the basis of sex in federally funded health programs and activities under Section 1557 of the Affordable Care Act, 42 U.S.C. § 18116 (“Section 1557”).
Ms. Gordon, Ms. Mayers, and Ms. Natividad are transgender women. At all times relevant to this Complaint, Plaintiffs are or were enrolled in health insurance plans offered, underwritten, or administered by Aetna (hereafter, “Aetna plans”). Ms. Gordon, a 42-year-old Nebraska resident, was covered at all relevant times by an Aetna plan under the Federal Employee Health Benefits (“FEHB”) program available to her through her tribal employer. Ms. Mayers, a 51-year-old Alaska resident, and Ms. Natividad, a 48-year-old Pennsylvania resident, have Aetna-administered plans offered by their respective employers.
Plaintiffs’ Aetna plans generally provide coverage for medically necessary surgery—including facial reconstructive surgery for diagnoses other than gender dysphoria—but categorically exclude GAFR when intended to treat gender dysphoria in t
note: source-backed benefits example from live index

To Whom It May Concern,
I am writing to appeal the denial of coverage for my medically necessary facial feminization surgery for the period of 04/11/2024 to 04/11/2025, as outlined in your letter dated 03/12/2024. The claim, associated with CPT codes 14040, 14301, 14302, 15773, 21121, 21137, 21138, 21139, and 21209, was unjustly denied under the pretext that the procedures are not medically necessary, an assertion starkly contradicted by a plethora of medical studies and legal precedents.
1. Medical Necessity and Personal Safety:
Your denial overlooks the vital role of facial feminization surgery in the lives of individuals diagnosed with gender dysphoria. This surgery is essential not only for my psychological well-being but also for my physical safety, given the prevalent discrimination and violence against transgender women.
Significant studies validate the necessity and efficacy of these surgeries:
Park RH et al. (2022) highlighted the long-term benefits of gender-affirming surgery, underscoring its life-saving potential (Ann Plast Surg. 89(4):431-436).
Dubov A, Fraenkel L (2018) discussed the ethical implications of gatekeeping in transgender health, emphasizing the medical necessity of facial feminization surgery (Am J Bioeth. 18(12):3-9).
Hauc SC et al. (2022) pointed out

This is usually at the end of the policy. It should include an address and the name of the administrator that the appeal should be addressed to.  If the denial letter and your plan policy give you different addresses, or you cannot find this information, please call your health plan or your HR department if you get your health insurance from your employer.  Information for the member section should be found in your denial letter. summary:
Health Insurance Claim Denied? See What Insurers Said Behind the Scenes

by Maya Miller, ProPublica, with additional reporting by Patrick Rucker, The Capitol Forum, and David Armstrong, ProPublica

ProPublica is a Pulitzer Prize-winning investigative newsroom. Sign up for The Big Story newsletter to receive stories like this one in your inbox.

Series: Uncovered:How the Insurance Industry Denies Coverage to Patients

Health insurers reject millions of claims for treatment every year in America. Corporate insiders, recordings and internal emails expose the system and its harm.

When a health insurance company is deciding whether to pay for your medical treatment, the company generates a file around your claim. All the records associated with your case should be part of your file. This includes documents explaining the reasons your claim was denied.

You have a right to see this file. Federal regulations require most health insurance plans to give people an opportunity to review documents related to their claim for free. So if your insurer talks to your doctor, if a nurse takes notes, or if two people speak about it on the phone, all of those records should be available to y

Subject: Appeal for Denial of Facial Feminization Surgery for Lexa [Your Last Name] (Claim Reference Number: 240311042646)
To Whom It May Concern,
I am writing to appeal the denial of coverage for my medically necessary facial feminization surgery. This procedure is a critical component of the treatment for my diagnosed Gender Dysphoria, and denying coverage for it ignores the medically recognized standards of care, the weight of scholarly research, and significant legal precedents.
1. Medical Necessity and Scholarly Research:
The necessity of facial feminization surgery for individuals suffering from Gender Dysphoria is well-documented. Studies such as “Facial Feminization Surgery: The Ethics of Gatekeeping in Transgender Health” (Am J Bioeth, 2020) highlight its importance for psychological well-being and social integration. Another significant study, “Psychological Functioning and Quality of Life in Transgender Candidates for Surgery,” (Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery, 2019), supports surgical intervention as a means to improve the quality of life and mental health outcomes for transgender women.
2. Personal Impact and Safety Considerations:
Being able to navigate everyday life and be recognized by others as a woman is not only medically necessary for me, Lexa [Your Last Name], but it can also present an issue of basic safety, especially in light of the staggeringly high rates of discrimination and violence against transgender

summary:
To Whom It May Concern,
I am writing in response to your request for additional information regarding my billing dispute for the following charge:
Merchant: VIVID SEATS *NEW YORK
Amount: $781.26
Date of Charge: September 18, 2025 (approximate)
I am disputing this charge because I did not receive the merchandise or services associated with this transaction and did not knowingly participate in or authorize the purchase.
Description of the Dispute
I did not knowingly place an order with Vivid Seats and was not aware of this charge at the time it occurred. I did not receive any tickets, confirmations, QR codes, emails, or event details related to this transaction. As of today, no merchandise or services have been provided to me for the amount charged.
The charge appeared during a broader account compromise incident on September 18, 2025, during which my financial information was misused. I became aware of the Vivid Seats charge only after reviewing my account activity and immediately took steps to secure my accounts.
A police report documenting this incident and the related unauthorized charges was filed with the Anchorage Police Department.
Police Report Number: 250506465
Merchant Contact / Cancellation
Because I did not knowingly place this order and had no order confirmation, ticket details, or transaction reference information, I was unable to cancel the transaction or return merchandise under the merchant’s policy. I did not have sufficient information to contact the merchant regarding a specific order or cancellation.

For a description of covered securities, see the Instructions for Form 8949. For a taxable covered security acquired at a premium, unless you notified the payer in writing in accordance with Regulations section 1.6045-1(n)(5) that you did not want to amortize the premium under section 171, or for a tax-exempt covered security acquired at a premium, your payer must generally report either (1) a net amount of interest that reflects the offset of the amount of interest paid to you by the amount of premium amortization allocable to the payment(s), or (2) a gross amount for both the interest paid to you and the premium amortization allocable to the payment(s). If you did notify your payer that you did not want to amortize the premium on a taxable covered security, then your payer will only report the gross amount of interest paid to you. For a noncovered security acquired at a premium, your payer is only required to report the gross amount of interest paid to you. Box 10. summary:
The deductible and coinsurance amounts noted above are estimates based on the policy information given to us by your insurance carrier (i.e. whether or not your deductible has been met). These amounts do not include anesthesia fees or pathology fees. These are separate fees that you may receive a bill for after surgery.
The amount due to the Gender Confirmation Center, Vista Surgery Center, or St. Francis Memorial Hospital following surgery may be significantly more or less than what was collected prior to surgery.
If we collected more than your portion, we will refund your credit card for the amount overpaid once we received payment from your insurance company.
If there is a balance due, you will receive a statement from the billing offices of either the Gender Confirmation Center, Vista Surgery Center and/or St. Francis Memorial Hospital.
If you do receive a bill, please contact your insurance plan, as they will be best equipped to help you. If your insurance plan is unable to help you, please contact the billing office of the party that sent the statement (Gender Confirmation Center, Vista Surgery Center and/or St. Francis Memorial Hospital).
Please note, once your claim has been processed by your insurance, you will receive documentation from your insurance company explaining your balance, which is called an Explanation of Benefits. You can use this document to verify either a statement or a refund.
By signing below, you acknowledge that you have read and understood the above.

summary:
CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY
Purpose
To assist the directors, officers and others in Identity to identify, evaluate, and resolve conflicts of interest.  While there is no federal legal requirement that an organization have a conflict of interest policy, Identity, its directors and officers may be subject to liability, and its exempt status may be at risk, if Identity enters into transactions that result in improper financial benefit to persons affiliated with Identity. Additionally, the IRS requires public charities to disclose on their annual information return whether they have a conflict of interest policy that meets certain requirements. This policy is designed to address these matters and is also designed to be used with a code ethics that further describes the policies and values of Identity.
Covered Persons
This conflict of interest policy of Identity, Inc. (Identity) is adopted by the board of directors and is applicable to all current directors, officers, as well as employees, volunteers, and independent contractors.
Affiliated Party
“Affiliated party” means a member of the Covered Person’s family (including spouses (and their siblings), lineal ancestors and descendants, spouses of lineal descendants, siblings (and their spouses and children), and domestic partners), or any entity in which the Covered Person (or any affiliated party) is a director, officer, or has a beneficial interest of more than 5%.
General 
All directors, officers and staff owe a duty of loyalty to Identity. The duty of loyalty requires that they exercise their powers in good faith and in the be

version: '3.8'

services:
  postgres:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    container_name: tichuml-db
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: tichuml
      POSTGRES_USER: tichuml
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: tichuml_dev
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U tichuml"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

volumes:
  pgdata:

summary:
# Repo Notes

## Telemetry Commands

- `npm run sim -- --games 10 --provider local --telemetry true --strict-telemetry false`
- `npm run sim -- --games 10 --provider server_heuristic --telemetry true --strict-telemetry false --backend-url http://127.0.0.1:4310`
- `npm run telemetry:replay`
- `npx tsx apps/sim-runner/src/telemetry/replay.ts`

## Telemetry Troubleshooting

- Live simulator/controller telemetry must stay non-blocking. If runs slow down,
  inspect `apps/sim-runner/src/telemetry/async-telemetry.ts` first and verify
  remote POSTs are still queue-backed instead of running inline with gameplay.
- Check `.runtime/telemetry/pending/` for durable local fallback files when the
  backend is slow, down, or in endpoint backoff.
- Check `.runtime/telemetry/replayed/` after replay to confirm recovery instead
  of silently deleting historical spillover.
- Use `GET /api/telemetry/health` and controller runtime state
  `telemetry_runtime` to verify queue depth, endpoint status, next retry time,
  and the last failure reason.
- Treat `strict_telemetry=true` as a telemetry-debug setting only. Normal sim
  and controller runs should keep `strict_telemetry=false` so remote transport
  failures degrade to local NDJSON persistence instead of failing gameplay.

ATTORNEY REPRESENTATION AGREEMENT
I agree to Wardenski P.C., Transgender Legal Defense & Education Fund, Inc. (“TLDEF”), and Cohen Milstein Sellers & Toll PLLC (“Attorneys”) representing __________________________ (“I or me”) in a putative class action lawsuit against Aetna Life Insurance Co. and any related entities as needed (“Aetna”). This lawsuit is to resolve claims that Aetna has violated the Affordable Care Act (“ACA”) by discriminating against me and other transgender people on the basis of sex through its categorical exclusion of coverage for medically-necessary facial gender-affirming surgery (“FGAS”). TLDEF has announced its intent to merge with the National Center for Transgender Equality to form a new organization called Advocates for Transgender Equality Education Fund, Inc. This agreement will continue in effect after the merger.
SCOPE OF REPRESENTATION
Under this Agreement, I authorize my Attorneys to represent me in an investigation of and litigation against Aetna. I understand that my Attorneys will be representing myself and other Aetna plan holders with similar claims who Aetna excluded from coverage for FGAS. I further understand that my Attorneys will seek to pursue my claim and those of other individuals with similar claims in a putative class action. I also understand that my Attorneys have no obligation to represent me on an individual or collective basis on any subsequent appeal in this matter or in any other legal matter.  If an appeal becomes necessary, and I wish to retain my Attorneys to represent me in that appeal, the terms of that representa

The ACLU of Alaska will review your complaint and contact you by mail/email. Please know that while we review all complaints we receive, we may not be able to respond to your complaint quickly. Please keep in mind that lawsuits and administrative complaints must be filed within certain time periods. This office cannot provide you with legal advice on how much time you have until you run out of time to file suit. Therefore, it is important to understand that it is your responsibility to seek a legal opinion from a lawyer licensed to practice in the State of Alaska. The ACLU of AK also does not have the time or the staff to give general legal advice and professional ethics would not allow us to discuss a case with someone who is represented by counsel. If you have any questions about how to resolve your legal problem, you may wish to contact the Alaska Bar Association's Lawyer Referral Service at 272-0352 (1-800-770-9999, outside of Anchorage). Please note that neither this message nor receipt of your complaint implies any agreement to undertake representation on your behalf. Thank you, ACLU of Alaska http://www.acluak.org/

The Solutions Architect is responsible for researching, documenting, and proposing new solutions to solve ASD technology needs. The architect is expected to be part project manager, part systems analyst, part systems architect, part implementation designer, part implementer, and part technical trainer. The position has a retirement association with the Public Employees’ Retirement System (PERS). Job Requirements The following are required: 1. A bachelor's degree with significant emphasis in computer science. Equivalent experience, education, and training may be considered in lieu of the degree requirement. 2. Proficiency in current versions of district operating systems. 4. Minimum of five years of experience managing enterprise services. 5. Knowledge of support for enterprise operating systems and applications. 6. Knowledge of support for cross-domain, cross-industry, and cross-functional solutions. 7. Courts have also found that these types of exclusions are discriminatory and violate federal law.
It is the overwhelming consensus among major medical organizations that transition-related treatments, including counseling, hormone therapy and surgical treatments, are safe, effective, and medically necessary when clinically indicated to treat gender dysphoria. Template Letter from You to Your Employer or School Asking Them to Remove an Exclusion
[FOR SELF-FUNDED PLANS WITH EXCLUSIONS ONLY]
INSTRUCTIONS: 
Only use this template if you have a self-funded plan that has an exclusion. To understand whether you have a self-funded plan, and for resources on approaching your employer, please refer to this page.
Replace all the bold text with your information.
The section that says “WHY REMOVING THE EXCLUSION IS IMPORTANT FOR YOU AND OTHER EMPLOYERS OR STUDENTS” is optional. You can use the space to include additional information about why it would be important for you or other employers or students that employers/students have access to transition-related care, or to a specific procedure that is currently excluded. If discussing a specific procedure, it would be helpful to include statements from medical organizations or studies. You can refer to the health care provider’s letter page for more resources.
Keep in mind that you can consider reaching out to other colleagues who may want to co-sign the letter and support you in this process, including non-transgender colleagues.
If your employer or school continues to refuse to remove the exclusion after you send this letter, we strongly recommend that you reach out to a lawyer to discuss other options (you can refer to this list of legal resources).

summary:
# TichuML Specification (`SPEC.md`)

## 1. Project Overview

TichuML is a production-leaning Tichu platform that combines:

- a correct, deterministic Tichu rules engine
- a mature, responsive game interface
- smart AI/heuristic players with team-aware behavior
- canonical telemetry for replay, debugging, and ML/simulation
- simulation tooling for large-scale policy evaluation

This project is not just a card game UI. It is a full system composed of:

- game engine
- match orchestration
- reactive frontend
- telemetry pipeline
- replay/debug tooling
- simulation harness

The goal is to build a modern, internal-alpha-quality Tichu platform that is testable, extensible, and usable for both live play and AI experimentation.

---

## 2. Product Goals

### Primary goals

1. Build a correct Tichu engine with deterministic, serializable state transitions.
2. Build a mature React/Vite/TypeScript UI that feels like a modern digital tabletop client.
3. Build smart AI players using layered heuristics rather than primitive random or naive play.
4. Capture canonical append-only telemetry for every decision point.
5. Support deterministic replay and AI decision inspection.
6. Support high-volume simulations for policy evaluation and future ML work.

### Definition of success

The project succeeds when it can:

- run full legal games of Tichu without soft locks
- support human + AI or AI-only matches
- expose a polished interface with responsive layout
- replay prior games from telemetry
- explain AI decisions in debug mode
- run large simulation batches with queryable results

---

## 3.

Broad Remarks Psychotherapy and psychological testing have risks and benefits. Talking about problems can cause uncomfortable feelings like grief and anger. On the other hand, mental health intervention has been shown to result in better relationships, solutions to specific problems, and improved mood. Intervention varies depending on the personalities of the clinician and patient, the particular problems at hand, and the methods of intervention used. Psychotherapy requires active and truthful engagement by the patient during sessions, and effortful practice between sessions. Clinicians at Alaska Telepsychology cannot guarantee you a favorable outcome. Our first few sessions will involve an evaluation of your needs. By the end of this evaluation, your clinician will offer initial diagnostic impressions and a treatment plan in the case of therapy, or will outline an assessment plan in the case of testing. If you and your clinician mismatch, you will be provided with referrals to practitioners better suited to help you. By signing here, I acknowledge and accept the potential risks and benefits of mental health intervention, as well as my patient responsibilities, and I consent to participate in this form of treatment. Scott, LMFT Aurora Sidney-Ando, LPC Gwen Perry-Crawford, LCSW Kierra Brecht, PhD Stephanie Birdsall, PMHNP CONFIDENTIAL PSYCHOLOGICAL EVALUATION SEPTEMBER 2023 NAME: Kay Mayers DATE OF BIRTH: 05/19/72 AGE: 51 years, 3 months REASON FOR REFERRAL Kay Mayers of Anchorage, Alaska, requested a psychological evaluation to determine whether she experiences Autism Spectrum Disorder and to formulate an actionable treatment plan. She has previously been diagnosed with ADHD, Anxiety, and Depression. Kay transitioned from male to female at age 50. Her dead name is "Keif" and for the purpose of this evaluation is referred to as Kay and she/her. BACKGROUND Family of Origin. From infancy through age 8, Kay was raised in the back of an automotive parts store by her married parents, Pamela and Charles. When she was 8, her parents divorced and her mother began work as a cook in a county jail. Kay never again saw her father. Her mother remarried, to an inmate named Mark. Mark was released from jail and lived with Pamela and Kay for the remainder of Kay's childhood. Childhood Experiences. " My school experience can be summarized as awful! No social skills. Fights." Kay reports that she "threw tantrums." She had no dependable friends. "I never fit in with the other boys. I got along better with the girls in the neighborhood." Kay earned generally poor grades. She excelled in academics only when interested in the topic at hand, as with world history

summary:
Weekly Study Plan Worksheet
Using the blank calendar on the next page and following the steps below, plan your weekly study schedule.
Block oﬀ existing obligations this week (i.e. work hours, childcare, family meal times, doctor’s appointment, etc)
Plan your fun ﬁrst! To help motivate your study time, schedule your distractions, exercise and relaxation time. This will give you something to look forward to.
Clearly mark any due dates or assignment deadlines, and use the GOALS list to remind yourself of the top tasks you need to complete this week or next.
Focus on tasks not time, and be as speciﬁc as possible. Review the activities you need to complete this week, estimate the time required for the task, and create a block for that task. For example, 25 minutes: watch video lecture, 25 minutes: create a practice test, 15 minutes: draft an outline for essay.
Use the pomodoro technique! When you begin a scheduled study block, work for 25-minute sessions followed by short breaks to keep your focus and energy going.
Pomodoro Technique
Pomodoro sets aside time for focusing on a speciﬁc task and rewards you with small chunks of time to be used for short breaks. There are six steps in the original technique:
Decide on the task to be done.
Set a timer for 25 minutes.
Work on the task.
End work when the timer rings and put a checkmark on a piece of paper.
If you have fewer than four checkmarks, take a short break (3–5 minutes), then go to step 2.
After four pomodoros, take a longer break (15–30 minutes), reset your checkmark count to zero, then go to step 1.

summary:
Chapter 1 – Ancient Conceptions Quiz
Question 1
The Dharmashastras are the sacred law books of the ______ religion that describe four stages of a man’s life.
Buddhist
Christian
Jewish
Hindu
Correct answer: Hindu
The Dharmashastras are key texts of the Hindu religion that lay out the four āśramas (stages of life) for men—student, householder, forest dweller and renouncer. These texts form part of Hindu sacred literature and emphasize duties at each life stage.
Question 2
According to the Jewish holy book, ______ is the age of moral responsibility.
18
10
8
13
Correct answer: 13
In Jewish tradition a boy becomes morally and religiously responsible at his bar mitzvah (age 13) and a girl at her bat mitzvah (age 12). The age of 13 thus marks the assumption of adult duties in Judaism.
Question 3
The Dharmashastras, the Talmud, and the conception of life proposed by Solon ______.
were written within a year of one another
see the final stages of life as a time of withdrawal and decline
are all sacred law books of the Hindu religion
each divide the life span into 5‑year periods
Correct answer: see the final stages of life as a time of withdrawal and decline
Although these texts and ideas come from different cultures, they all view the end of life as a period of retreat and decline. The Hindu Dharmashastras, the Jewish Talmud and Solon’s stages of life emphasize a withdrawal from worldly roles in later years.

the oral stage lasts from about ages 3 to 6
children experience the anal stage that leads them to s

[DATE]
RE: [PATIENT NAME]
Birth date: 00/00/0000
SS#: 111-11-1111
To Whom It May Concern:
I,  [NAME OF PROVIDER] , am the provider of  [NAME OF PATIENT], with whom I have a doctor/patient relationship, and for whom I provide psychiatric treatment. 
In my medical opinion, [NAME OF PATIENT] is a woman. [NAME OF PATIENT] has had appropriate clinical treatment and has successfully completed her transition from male to female. 
When [NAME OF PATIENT] is identified in a manner on her passport, driver’s license and social security records in a way that does not accurately reflect her gender, it causes confusion and bias, leading to unnecessary stressors for my patient.  Correctly identifying [NAME OF PATIENT] as female will alleviate unnecessary mental distress and improve the patient’s mental health.
I declare under penalty of perjury under the laws of the United States that the forgoing is true and correct. 
Sincerely,
[NAME OF PROVIDER]
[LICENSE NUMBER]

summary: Application for a Social Security Card Applying for a Social Security Card is free! USE THIS APPLICATION TO: ● Apply for an original Social Security card ● Apply for a replacement Social Security card ● Change or correct information on your Social Security number record IMPORTANT: You MUST provide a properly completed application and the required evidence before we can process your application. We can only accept original documents or documents certified by the custodian of the original record. Notarized copies or photocopies which have not been certified by the custodian of the record are not acceptable. We will return any documents submitted with your application. For assistance call us at 1-800-772-1213 or visit our website at www.socialsecurity.gov. Original Social Security Card To apply for an original card, you must provide at least two documents to prove age, identity, and U.S. citizenship or current lawful, work-authorized immigration status. If you are not a U.S. citizen and do not have DHS work authorization, you must prove that you have a valid non-work reason for requesting a card. See page 2 for an explanation of acceptable documents. NOTE: If you are age 12 or older and have never received a Social Security number, you must apply in person. Replacement Social Security Card To apply for a replacement card, you must provide one document to prove your identity. If you were born outside the U.S., you must also provide documents to prove your U.S. citizenship or current, lawful, work-authorized status. See page 2 for an explanation of acceptable documents. summary:
Certification of Health Care Provider for Employee's Serious Health Condition under the Family and Medical Leave Act 
DO NOT SEND COMPLETED FORM TO THE DEPARTMENT OF LABOR. RETURN TO THE PATIENT. 
U.S. Department of Labor Wage and Hour Division 
~WH✩ 
WAGE AND HOUR DIVISION 
OMB Control Number: 1235-0003 Expires: 6/30/2023 
The Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA) provides that an employer may require an employee seeking FMLA protections because of a need for leave due to a serious health condition to submit a medical certification issued by the employee's health care provider. 29 U.S.C. §§ 2613, 2614(c)(3); 29 C.F.R. § 825.305. The employer must give the employee at least 15 calendar days to provide the certification. If the employee fails to provide complete and sufficient medical certification, his or her FMLA leave request may be denied. 29 C.F.R. § 825.313. Information about the FMLA may be found on the WHD website at www.dol.gov/agencies/whd/fmla. 
SECTION I-EMPLOYER 
Either the employee or the employer may complete Section I. While use of this form is optional, this form asks the health care provider for the information necessary for a complete and sufficient medical certification, which is set out at 29 C.F.R. § 825.306. You may not ask the employee to provide more information than allowed under the FMLA regulations, 29 C.F.R. §§ 825.306-825.308. Additionally, you may not request a certification for FMLA leave to bond with a healthy newborn child or a child placed for adoption or foster care. 
Employers must generally maintain records and documents relating to medica

summary:
Influencing Health Behaviors
“Alone we can do so little; together we can do so much.” — Helen Keller
Introduction
The Social Ecological Model (SEM) explains how layered systems shape health behaviors—from the individual to relationships, organizations, communities, and policy/structural forces. Using SEM with interprofessional practice (IPP) helps teams design coordinated interventions that are more effective, equitable, and sustainable.
Healthy Alaskans 2030
• Alaska’s 10-year improvement plan with 15 priority topics and 30 measurable objectives.
• Co-led by the State of Alaska and the Alaska Native Tribal Health Consortium; strong emphasis on equity.
• Relevant focus: reducing the incidence rate of gonorrhea among young adults (18–24).
Teamwork (IPP)
Value-based care depends on shared values, clear roles, respectful communication, and adaptive teamwork. IPP reduces errors and duplication, coordinates care, and improves outcomes by helping professionals learn about, from, and with each other.
Three-Step Method:
1. Identify the health problem (e.g., high STI rates in 18–24-year-olds).
2. Map barriers at each SEM level.
3. Design multi-level interventions so each level reinforces the others.
SEM Levels
Individual Level
Health Educator — one-to-one education on risk, testing, and prevention; brief counseling; distribute condoms and culturally appropriate materials.
Interpersonal Level
Social Worker / Counselor — skills for partner/family communication; peer groups; address stigma and support healthy choices.
Organizational Level
Nurse & Clinic Partners — youth-friendly clinic

summary:
Dear Honorable Representative Mary Peltoa,
I am writing to you today to express my concern about the lack of coverage for necessary medical procedures for transgender individuals in Alaska. As you may know, transgender people face significant barriers to healthcare, including high costs, harassment, and a lack of trained providers. In addition, nearly one-third of transgender people report being refused medical care or harassed by medical professionals because they are transgender.
It is unacceptable that transgender people in Alaska are being denied access to necessary medical procedures due to discrimination by state and local governments.Very specifically state and local government entities are very specifically excluding medical coverage for transgender related surgeries.
Also the current state and local administration is hostile to transgender/non-binary, and specifically targeting transgender youth, adults and denying education of these important issues to future generations.
I urge you to take action to address this issue and ensure that all Alaskans have access to the healthcare they need and are not subject to discrimination by state, and local government.
Thank you for your attention to this important matter.
Sincerely,
Kay Mayers
907-575-6739

summary:
Application to Amend a Birth Record    
Introduction  
Use the Application to Amend a Birth Record (VS 24B) form to:  
• Amend a Birth Record (See checklist on page 3)  
  
▪ Correct spelling errors  
▪ Add the child’s first, middle, or last name to blank fields  
  
▪ Correct the sex field, date, time, or place of birth of the child  
▪ Add a parent’s new name after a court order or naturalization process  
  
▪ Correct parents’ place or date of birth  
  
• Change Child’s Sex Field to Reflect Gender Identity (See checklist on page 7)  
  
• Change Parent Designation to Reflect Gender Identity on Child’s Birth Certificate  (See checklist on page 11)  
  
• Please see page 15 for a list of frequently asked questions and answers.  • For other amendment types, please visit the CDPH-VR website at www.cdph.ca.gov.  
Mailing Address  
Mail completed application and appropriate fee(s) to the address below:  
CDPH – Vital Records  
MS 5105  
P.O. Box 997410  
Sacramento, CA 95899-7410  
• Amended certificates are mailed via USPS First Class and do not contain a tracking  number.  
• You may provide a self-addressed prepaid envelope that utilizes tracking with your  request.

summary:
# TichuML

A production-minded full-stack Tichu game platform for play, simulation, telemetry, and future ML training.

## Architecture

```
┌─────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐
│   Client     │◄──►│   Server     │◄──►│  PostgreSQL  │
│  React+Pixi  │    │  Express API │    │  Telemetry   │
└──────┬───────┘    └──────┬───────┘    └──────────────┘
       │                   │
       ▼                   ▼
┌─────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐
│   Engine     │    │   Sim/CLI    │
│  Pure Logic  │    │  AI Policies │
└─────────────┘    └──────────────┘
```

### Packages

| Package | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `@tichuml/shared` | Shared types, action definitions, telemetry schemas |
| `@tichuml/engine` | Pure deterministic game engine — no DB, no React |
| `@tichuml/server` | Express API, DB persistence, simulation orchestration |
| `@tichuml/client` | React + PixiJS frontend — play client & debug viewer |
| `@tichuml/sim` | AI policies, batch simulation runner, CLI tools |

## Quick Start

```bash
# Install dependencies
npm install

# Start Postgres (optional — app works in-memory without it)
docker compose up -d

# Run migrations (requires Postgres)
npm run migrate

# Start dev server + client
npm run dev

# Or start individually:
npm run dev:server  # API on :3001
npm run dev:client  # Client on :5173
```

## Simulation

```bash
# Run AI vs AI simulation
npm run sim -- --games 20 --seed 42 --target 500

# Specify policies per seat
npm run sim -- --games 100 --p0 heuristic --p1 random --p2 heuristic --p3 random

# Available policies: random, heuristic
`

summary:
services:
  postgres:
    image: postgres:16
    container_name: ${POSTGRES_CONTAINER_NAME:-tichu-postgres}
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: ${POSTGRES_USER:-tichu}
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-tichu_dev_password}
      POSTGRES_DB: ${POSTGRES_DB:-tichu}
    ports:
      - "${POSTGRES_PORT:-54329}:5432"
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test:
        [
          "CMD-SHELL",
          "pg_isready -U ${POSTGRES_USER:-tichu} -d ${POSTGRES_DB:-tichu}"
        ]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 20

volumes:
  postgres_data:

summary:
# tichuml Windows Startup Scripts

Default repo root: `C:\tichu\tichuml`

Canonical local database identity:

```text
POSTGRES_CONTAINER_NAME=tichu-postgres
POSTGRES_USER=tichu
POSTGRES_PASSWORD=tichu_dev_password
POSTGRES_DB=tichu
POSTGRES_PORT=54329
DATABASE_URL=postgres://tichu:tichu_dev_password@localhost:54329/tichu
PG_BOOTSTRAP_URL=postgres://tichu:tichu_dev_password@localhost:54329/postgres
```

## First install

```powershell
Set-ExecutionPolicy -Scope Process Bypass -Force
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts\install-backend.ps1
```

## Daily start/update

```powershell
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts\start-backend.ps1
```

## Status

```powershell
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts\status-backend.ps1
```

## Stop

```powershell
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts\stop-backend.ps1
```

## Reset mismatched Postgres

If `tichu-postgres` was created with the old identity
`POSTGRES_USER=postgres` or `POSTGRES_DB=tichuml`, the start scripts fail loudly.
Reset the local container and volume:

```powershell
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts\reset-db.ps1
```

## Clear app data without recreating Postgres

Issue [#65](https://github.com/NeonButrfly/tichuml/issues/65) tracks the
canonical in-place data clear flow. This keeps the existing database, container,
schema, roles, extensions, and migration bookkeeping intact while truncating
application data tables only:

```powershell
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts\clear-db.ps1 --yes
```

## Capture a restoreable DB snapshot plus diagnostics

summary:
# TichuML

TichuML is a deterministic Tichu platform monorepo with:

- an authoritative TypeScript rules engine
- headless AI round execution and telemetry capture
- a responsive React table UI with table-editor tooling
- a local backend for entropy, telemetry ingest, decision routing, and replay reads
- replay, debugging, and simulation foundations

The repository no longer reflects a Milestone 0-only scaffold. Historical milestone labels still exist in a few runtime constants and tests for compatibility, but the working project has advanced well beyond the bootstrap stage.

## Current Workspace

- `apps/web` - live game client, responsive table UI, table editor, dialogs, and local app state
- `apps/server` - HTTP JSON API for health, entropy, telemetry, replay reads, and server-side heuristic routing
- `apps/sim-runner` - deterministic headless round execution for batch and integration flows
- `packages/engine` - authoritative rules engine, deterministic shuffle, trick resolution, scoring
- `packages/ai-heuristics` - heuristic AI decision policy and explanation output
- `packages/telemetry` - append-only decision/event telemetry helpers
- `packages/shared` - shared metadata, seed provenance types, and cross-package helpers
- `packages/ui-kit` - reusable UI primitives
- `infra/db` - migrations and migration runner
- `docker-compose.yml` - local Postgres compose stack
- `ml` - LightGBM feature building, export, training, and inference scripts
- `docs` - architecture, milestone history, telemetry, product, UI, and workflow notes
- `tests` - integration and replay-oriented va

I hope this email finds you well. I recently received and reviewed my performance evaluation, and I would like to take this opportunity to address some concerns and provide additional context to ensure a fair and accurate assessment of my performance. I believe it is essential to engage in open and constructive dialogue in order to promote growth and improvement within the organization.
1.	Lack of Specific Examples: I noticed that the review lacks specific instances or examples to support the negative feedback provided. It would be immensely helpful if you could provide specific instances where my performance fell short or where improvement is needed. This will enable me to have a clear understanding of the areas that require my attention and allow me to devise effective strategies for improvement.
2.	Incomplete Picture: The performance review appears to present an incomplete picture of my overall performance. It fails to acknowledge my contributions to specific projects or achievements. I would appreciate if these achievements are given due consideration and reflected accurately in my evaluation.
3.	Lack of Training and Guidance: It is important to note that I have not received adequate training or clear guidance on certain aspects of my responsibilities. It would greatly benefit my performance if I were provided with additional resources, training opportunities, or mentoring to enhance my skills in those specific areas. summary: 09/11/2024 Kay Mayers 1941 East View Dr Anchorage, AK 99504-2959 Dear Kay Mayers Thank you for your interest in the Public Access to Court Electronic Records (PACER) service. In order to activate your search capabilities in PACER, please follow the steps provided below: 1. Go to pacer.gov and click on Manage My Account. 2. To log in, enter the username and password you set up during registration. 3. Click the Activate Case Search Privileges link under the Settings tab. 4. Enter your authentication token: ZIUP-HK4I-7T4X 5. Click Submit. All registered agencies or individuals are charged a $.10 per page fee for access to PACER. Statements are generated and distributed quarterly, and payment is due each quarter. Further access to the service will be restricted until outstanding balances are cleared. By logging into PACER, you acknowledge you have read, understood, and agree to the terms and conditions on the reverse side of this letter. If you have any questions regarding your account, please contact the PACER Service Center by phone at (800) 676-6856 or by email at pacer @ psc.uscourts.gov. Visit pacer.gov for the latest information, including FAQs and quarterly newsletters. Sincerely, David Stone ; Chief, PACER Support Branch U.S. Courts - PACER P.O. Box5208 Portland, OR 97208-5208 Fee Information - Last Updated: Jamary 1, 2020 There is no registration fee. ]

    "decline_grand_tichu",
    "call_tichu",
    "select_pass",
    "pass_turn",
    "play_cards",
    "assign_dragon_trick",
    "advance_phase",
]

    "single",
    "pair",
    "triple",
    "straight",
    "pair-sequence",
    "full-house",
    "bomb-four-kind",
    "bomb-straight",
    "dog",
    "unknown",
]

SEATS = ["seat-0", "seat-1", "seat-2", "seat-3"]
RELATIONS = ["self", "partner", "left_opponent", "right_opponent", "none"]
URGENCY_MODES = [
    "normal",
    "opponent_near_out",
    "self_near_out",
    "partner_support",
    "endgame",
]


def get_partner_seat(seat: str) -> str:
    seat_index = int(seat.split("-")[-1])
    return f"seat-{(seat_index + 2) % 4}"


def get_left_opponent_seat(seat: str) -> str:
    seat_index = int(seat.split("-")[-1])
    return f"seat-{(seat_index + 3) % 4}"


def get_right_opponent_seat(seat: str) -> str:
    seat_index = int(seat.split("-")[-1])
    return f"seat-{(seat_index + 1) % 4}"


def phase_alias(phase: str) -> str:
    return "trick_play" if phase == "play" else phase


def relation_for_seat(reference_seat: str, candidate_seat: str | None) -> str:
    if not candidate_seat:
        return "none"
    if candidate_seat == reference_seat:
        return "self"
    if candidate_seat == get_partner_seat(reference_seat):
        return

Professor Phil Jordan
 Meets on Blackboard
University of Alaska Anchorage

Email: pajordan@alaska.edu
Office hours: By appointment
Students should feel free to contact the professor regarding any questions you might have concerning the course. When contacting me please include your Name, Student ID Number, Course Name, Section Number, Current Semester, and preferred contact information (email, phone, etc.)
Regular and Substantive Interaction
This course is fully asynchronous and has no set meeting times; you will have course activities and due dates throughout the term. I will substantively interact with you through regular [class discussion boards, announcements that provide class feedback and direction, individual feedback on your projects, and optional study sessions].
Student Contact Information
Realize that the only contact information we have for you is the information (name, email, etc.) that is listed on UAonline and Blackboard.
If there has been a change such as marriage or divorce and the change has not been processed properly through the Office of Admissions and Records then there can be a monumental problem as we will not be able to contact you and your grades might not be recorded properly.
Therefore, make sure we have all your updated information so there won’t be any problems with you obtaining the grade you have worked so hard to earn. 
Instructional Goals
Orient students toward the historical roots, dominant perspectives, and, major issues in the scientific study of lifespan development.

summary:
Create a 60-second cinematic video titled “Public Health Nurse Recruitment Pitch.”
Use warm, golden morning lighting, soft cinematic visuals, and an inspirational female narrator. 
Add smooth transitions, motivational background music, and clear on-screen captions for each scene. 
Keep the tone professional, compassionate, and hopeful.
Include the following scenes:
Scene 1 (0:00–0:10) — The Calling
Visual: A nurse walking toward a small community clinic in the morning sunlight.
On-screen text: “Some nurses heal in hospitals; public health nurses heal entire communities.”
Voiceover: “Some nurses heal in hospitals, but public health nurses heal entire communities — meeting people where they live, work, and grow.”
Scene 2 (0:11–0:20) — The Role
Visual: A nurse talking with families and patients in a welcoming clinic.
On-screen text: “Promoting wellness. Preventing disease. Protecting populations.”
Voiceover: “Public Health Nurses promote wellness, prevent disease, and protect entire populations before illness even begins.”
Scene 3 (0:21–0:35) — Education and Growth
Visual: Nursing students studying together or practicing skills in training.
On-screen text: “From nursing foundations to public health leadership.”
Voiceover: “Start with nursing foundations, then grow into public health leadership — combining clinical skills with community care.”


Scene 4 (0:36–0:50) — Impact and Demand
Visual: Nurses helping at health fairs, community events, and home visits.
On-screen text: “High demand. Real impact. Beyond hospitals.”
Voiceover: “Public Health Nurses are in high demand — makin

summary:
Middle Adulthood Quiz Questions
The following questions and answers are copied from the Pearson Revel “Emotional and Self-Development” and “The Social and Cultural Contexts of Middle Adulthood” quizzes.
Quiz: Emotional and Self-Development
Question 1: What did the study comparing self-development of Americans and South Koreans in their 50s find?
There were no differences in self-development between Americans and South Koreans.
South Koreans were higher in traits such as self-acceptance and autonomy.
Americans were higher in traits such as self-acceptance and autonomy. (Correct)
Americans were higher in self-acceptance but South Koreans were higher on autonomy.
Question 2: Regarding the midlife crisis, research consistently indicates that ________.
the claim of a universal midlife crisis is false. (Correct)
the majority of women experience this as a result of the empty nest, but it is much less likely to be experienced by men because they are more likely to work outside the home.
the majority of the American population experiences it either in their 40s or 50s.
only those from ethnic minority backgrounds experience it because of more stress and discrimination than their counterparts from majority backgrounds.
Question 3: Which of Erikson’s crises focuses on people’s desire to “live on” after they have died and to help those younger than they so that the effects of their time on earth will be evident when they are gone?
Generativity vs. stagnation (Correct)
Integrity vs. despair
Autonomy vs. doubt
Legacy vs. selfishness

OCRenforces federal civil rights laws which prohibit discrimination in the delivery of health and human services based onrace, color, national origin, disability, age, sex, religion, and the exercise of conscience, and also enforces the HealthInsurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA) Privacy, Security and Breach Notification Rules. We are in the process of reviewing your correspondence. We will complete our initial review as quickly as possible. If you have questions about the novel coronavirus, COVID-19, please go to the Centers for Disease Control websiteat https://www.cdc.gov. For additional information about OCR, including the complaint review process, and our HIPAA, civil rights, andconscience and religious freedom regulations, please see the following links: https://www.hhs.gov/ocr/complaints/index.htmlhttps://www.hhs.gov/hipaa/index.htmlhttps://www.hhs.gov/civil-rights/index.htmlhttps://www.hhs.gov/conscience/index.html If you have any additional questions or need a reasonable accommodation, please contact OCR’s CustomerResponse Center at 1-800-368-1019, Monday through Friday, 8:00 am to 6:00 pm, ET. These documents are for use when providing items and services to participants, beneficiaries, enrollees, or covered individua ls in group health plans or group or individua l health insurance coverage, including Federal Employees Health Benefits (FEHB) plans by either: • A nonparticipating provider or nonparticipating emergency facility when furnishing certain post-stabilizat ion services, or • A nonparticipating provider (or facility on behalf of the provider) when furnishing non- emergency services (other than ancillary services) at certain participating health care facilities. T hese documents provide the form and manner of the notice and consent documents specified by the Secretary of HHS under 45 CFR 149.410 and 149.420. HHS considers use of these documents in accordance with these instructions to be good faith compliance with the notice and consent requirements of section 2799B-2(d) of the PHS Act, provided that all other requirements are met. summary: HEALTH INSURANCE SPOUSAL AFFIDAVIT Dependent Verification The District requires that spouse s enroll in their employer’s health insurance as primary coverage to be eligible to enroll in the ASD health plan. The following SPOUSAL AFFIDAVIT FORM must be completed by all applicable parties and uploaded to c omplete the enrollment process. Failure to submit requested documents will impact your ability to enroll your spouse in the ASD health plan. Section A: To be complete by you Section B: To be completed by your named spouse. Section C: To be completed by your spouse’s employer to verify medical insurance coverage. *If your spouse is an ASD employee and has insurance through the ASD health plan or a health trust with their union, forward your form after Section B to the Benefits Department at benefitsdept@asdk12.org for assistance. Submitting your completed form: Once all sections are complete, u pload your form through the benefits portal , BenefitSpot. Refer to the dependent verification email from TRI-AD for further instructions. Do not send completed forms to the Benefits Department unless Section C applies to you. Questions on uploading documents should be directed to TRI-AD by phone at 877-874-3213 or email asdk12benefits@tri -ad.com. Questions/concerns on the spousal affidavit form should be directed to Benefits at benefitsdept@asdk12.org . Benefits Department 5530 E. Northern Lights Blvd • Anchorage, AK 99504 • 907-742-4200 ANCHORAGE SCHOOL DISTRICT SPOUSAL INSURANCE AFFIDAVIT To be eligible for coverage, your spouse must elect their employer's medical plan as primary

summary: What happens if don’t go through OE - force enrollment in medical if don’t go through process? 2022 Open Enrollment May 2nd – 20th This is your annual opportunity to change, elect, or waive your benefits. Please review the information in this packet to learn about open enrollment and the benefit changes for 2022. Open Enrollment elections will be effective July 1, 2022 through June 30, 2023. Elections cannot be changed until the next Open Enrollment unless you experience a ‘qualified status change’ such as a change in marital status, the birth of a baby or loss/gain of other coverage. What’s Changing Medical Plan • There are no changes to either medical plan. The PPO plan is closed to new enrollments. • There will be a change to the employee contributions on both medical plans. The new contributions will be: BenefitSpot - How to Enroll ASD has a new Benefits Administration Platform that went live on November 1, 2021. All regular ASD employees will login to BenefitSpot to sign-up for benefits within 45 days from their date of hire, report qualifying life events, make election changes during open enrollment, enter life insurance beneficiaries, find information on benefit providers and make other benefit related changes within this platform. Nearly all benefit activities will occur in BenefitSpot. The Benefits Department is available to support ASD staff in navigating benefit questions and any portal related issues. summary: MEDICINE & MUSIC Retreat with Anchorage Yoga Finca el Péndulo View booking and trip details here Your host Anchorage Yoga Mar 29 - Apr 4, 2026 Group size: 1-18 Finca el Péndulo About this trip Anchorage Yoga is excited to present an exclusive retreat—an offering like never before. This unique experience, with its profound transformative potential, focuses on healing through music and earth medicines. It's designed to guide you into the depths of your heart, where you can repair and reconnect with your most authentic self: love. This journey promises to inspire hope and ignite transformation. Anchorage Yoga's Medicine & Music Retreat will occur from March 29th to April 4th at Finca El Pendulo in Jalisco, México. A unique feature of this retreat is an optional music festival on the night of the 4th. This festival, showcasing incredible Bhakti musicians, provides an enchanting conclusion to our transformational retreat. It offers a unique opportunity to further immerse yourself in the healing power Trip Overview View booking and trip details here of music, a key element of our retreat's transformative potential. What's included Lodging 6 nights/ 7 days in at Finca el Péndulo All meals gourmet organic, farm-to-table vegetarian & meat options. Classes Multiple yoga, pranayama, and meditation classes daily Music Live music provided for all the ceremonies Ceremony Sacred cacao , & optional sweat lodge (Temazcal) Transfers Airport transportation to and from retreat center to Guadalajara (GDL) Airfare Travel Insurance Medical & Trip cancellation is highly recommended to protect you

summary: New Patient Cognitive Questionnaire Thank you for investing the time to complete this form. This questionnaire will provide valuable and important information about you and will give us a more thorough understanding of your difficulties. It is important that you complete it thoroughly and preferably before the time of your appointment as it requires some time that would otherwise be used for your evaluation. Please have a care giver or immediate family member assist you in filling out this questionnaire and we strongly recommend that you have a caregiver, immediate family member or close friend accompany you to your appointments. Professor Johnson
PHIL A201

The Problem of Evil: A Comparison of Stump and Rowe
During the first few weeks of this semester we have looked at several philosophical topics including logic, deductive reasoning, philosophy of religion, the problem of evil, and epistemology. Out of all of these, the problem of evil feels like one of the most difficult and uncomfortable. The basic issue is simple to state: how can an all-powerful and perfectly good God exist if there is so much suffering in the world? At first glance, those ideas do not seem to fit together easily. If God has both the power to prevent suffering and the desire to do so, then the continued presence of suffering seems like something that needs explaining.
Eleonore Stump argues that suffering does not automatically create a contradiction between God’s goodness and the existence of evil. In her view, we should not assume that goodness always means eliminating suffering right away. She suggests that suffering may serve a purpose, even if we cannot see that purpose clearly. Some forms of suffering might contribute to moral or spiritual growth. From this perspective, a perfectly good being might allow suffering if it leads to a greater good in the long run. Just because we do not see that good does not mean it is not there.
William Rowe approaches the issue differently. He does not claim that God and evil logically contradict each other. Instead, he argues that certain cases of suffering count as evidence against the existence of a perfectly good and all-powerful God. His well-known example o

summary:
Kay Mayers
Professor Johnson
PHIL A201
27 February 2026
The Problem of Evil: A Comparison of Stump and Rowe
During the first few weeks of this semester we have looked at several philosophical topics including logic, deductive reasoning, philosophy of religion, the problem of evil, and epistemology. One of the most persistent questions in philosophy of religion is whether the existence of evil can fit with the existence of an all-powerful and perfectly good God. At first glance, these ideas seem difficult to hold together. If God has both the power and the desire to prevent suffering, then the continued presence of suffering still demands some kind of explanation.
Eleonore Stump argues that suffering does not create a logical contradiction between God’s goodness and the reality of evil because suffering may serve purposes beyond our limited understanding. William Rowe, in “The Problem of Evil and Some Varieties of Atheism,” does not claim that God and evil cannot both be true. Instead, he argues that certain cases of suffering provide evidence against God’s existence. I will argue that while Stump blocks the claim that God and evil are logically incompatible, Rowe’s argument based on probability remains a serious challenge because the repeated appearance of apparently pointless suffering cannot simply be ignored.
Stump argues that suffering may serve a purpose even when that purpose is not immediately obvious to us. Goodness is not the same thing as eliminating all suffering. Some forms of suffering may contribute to moral or spiritual growth in ways that are hard to see fro

summary:
Kay Mayers
Professor Johnson
PHIL A201
1 March 2026
The Problem of Evil: A Logical and Evidential Comparison
During the first few weeks of this semester we have examined several philosophical topics including logic, deductive reasoning, philosophy of religion, the problem of evil, and epistemology. One of the most persistent questions that arises in philosophy of religion is whether the existence of evil is compatible with the existence of an all-powerful and perfectly good God. At first glance, these ideas seem difficult to hold together. If God has the power to prevent suffering and also has the desire to do so, then it becomes unclear why suffering continues to exist in the world at all.
In J. L. Mackie’s “Evil and Omnipotence,” he argues that the traditional conception of God is logically inconsistent when combined with the existence of evil. William Rowe, in his article “The Problem of Evil and Some Varieties of Atheism,” approaches the issue somewhat differently. Rowe does not argue that God and evil are logically incompatible, but instead that the existence of certain kinds of suffering makes God’s existence unlikely. In the first part of this essay I will compare Mackie’s logical problem of evil with Rowe’s evidential problem of evil. In the second part I will explain my own view.
Part I: Comparing Mackie and Rowe
Mackie’s argument can be expressed in deductive form. First, if God exists then God is omnipotent and perfectly good. Second, a perfectly good being would eliminate evil so far as it could. Third, an omnipotent being could eliminate any evil that is logica

summary: Testing Consent Form Patient Details Full name Date of birth MM/DD/YY Address line 1 Address line 2 City, State, Zip Email address Phone number (###) ###-#### Gender Preferred way to receive reminders (select one): 🔲 email 🔲 text. Purpose of Testing: To clarify the root of intractable problems (e.g., developmental disorders, mental health or learning problems) & to generate actionable treatment recommendations. Evaluators: Our evaluators are Cathy von Hippel, Ph.D., Sammy Mack, Ph.D. and Kierra Brecht, Ph.D. Typically two psychologists work on a single evaluation, combining professional acumen to provide more meaningful feedback. First Appointment: The purpose of the intake is to collect history and confirm the need for a psychological evaluation. For children, this is a parents-only meeting. Scheduling: Multiple appointments are scheduled within a short period. The time required for testing depends on the referral question and patient characteristics, such as ability to focus. To avoid misleading results caused by fatigue, we administer testing over multiple sessions. Collateral Information: As needed, we review prior evaluations, observe at school/in the community, review videos, and interview family members, educators, and involved health care professionals. Feedback: Verbal and written feedback are available within two weeks of the last day of testing. We write a report summarizing results, diagnoses, and treatment recommendations. We meet with children separate from parents to provide feedback geared towards their level of understanding. Page of 13 DocuSign Envelope ID

summary: KAY MAYERS Social Norms. We recommend that Kay read Mis: ner’s Guide to Excruciatingl Correct Behavior, or a comparable book of social instruction, to improve her awareness of social conventions. Continued Speech Therapy. We recommend that Kay continue to participate in speech-language therapy. Kay currently has little ability to “read” social situations and to identify her feelings, so needs help to develop these skills. In conversations, she struggles to articulate her thoughts and her responses are often non-linear; she needs strategies for responding concisely. summary: ic} IMAGING ASSOCIATES Expert Radiology. Exceptional Care. April 28, 2023 Kay Mayers 1941 E View Dr Anchorage, AK 99504 Dear Ms. Mayers: Good News! Your diagnostic mammography tomo performed on 04/27/2023 is considered ..._ normal. There are no areas of concern noted on your images. Based on your mammogram, your breast density is: Heterogeneously dense-majority of the breasts are dense with some areas of non-dense tissue Dense breast tissue is very common and is not abnormal. However, heterogeneously dense and extremely dense breast tissue can make it harder to see breast cancer on a mammogram. If you have heterogeneously or extremely dense breast tissue, please discuss with your healthcare provider whether you might benefit from additional supplemental in addition to your yearly mammogram. Current American College of Radiology Guidelines recommend screening mammography and breast physical examination every year beginning at the age of 40. Please continue to examine your breasts and report any changes to your healthcare provider without delay. Your breast imaging studies will become part of your medical file here at Imaging Associates, Anchorage for at least ten years. It is your responsibility to inform any new health care provider or mammography facility of the date and location of this examination. You will receive a reminder letter when it is time for your next routine mammogram, / / Sincerely, Brittany Osteen, MD Interpreting Radiologist R002292AK IAA - PB Imaging Associates - Anchorage aa Bb IMAGING 3650 Piper Street Ste A ASSOCIATES Anchorage, AK 99508 Phone #: (907)

My name is Keif (Kay) Mayers, and I am an employee at Anchorage School District. I am enrolled in the self-funded health plan, administered by Aetna. The denial of care for gender dysphoria (also known as transition-related care), a serious medical condition that affects many transgender people. According to the denial there is a specific exclusion for transgender related care.
I respectfully ask that the pre-authorization of coverage for gender dysphoria treatment be re-evaluated and approved.
An exclusion for transgender related care is discriminatory and not in compliance with federal law.

summary: MCG and Gender-Af rming Surgery: An Introduction Search our blog Search SUBSCRIBE TO OUR RSS FEED JOIN OUR MAILING LIST YOU MIGHT ALSO BE INTERESTED IN... MCG Health’s Efforts to Support Health Equity  MENU Blog Category List MCG and Support for NCQA Accreditation New Recommendations for Colorectal Cancer Screening AMBULATORY CARE ANALYTICS & STATISTICS BEHAVIORAL HEALTH CARE CARE STRATEGIES CASE MANAGEMENT CHRONIC CARE GOVERNMENT HEALTHCARE HEALTH IT HOME CARE INPATIENT SURGICAL CARE MCG CARE GUIDELINES MCG CLIENT FORUM MCG COMPANY CULTURE MCG NEWS MCG TRAINING MEDICARE COMPLIANCE MHEALTH & TELEMEDICINE POPULATION HEALTH QUALITY AND ACCREDITATION RECOVERY FACILITY CARE STRATEGIC PARTNERS UNCATEGORIZED MENU In MCG Health’s 27 edition, a new General Recovery Care guideline (GRG) was developed to collate gender-af rming surgeries and procedures into a single dedicated guideline. In prior editions, the surgeries were listed in the GRG that is most pertinent to the procedure. In the new Gender-Af rming Surgery or Procedure GRG, procedures are grouped as either a masculinization/defeminization procedure or a feminization/demasculinization procedure. Both groups include chest, genital, gonad, aesthetic (e.g., facial), and voice procedures. GENDER-AFFIRMING SURGERY: TERMINOLOGY AND BACKGROUND Accurate use of terminology is important when considering healthcare for transgender and gender-diverse (TGD) people. Gender identity refers to a person’s internal intrinsic sense of their own gender. Nonalignment or incompatibility between gender identity and the sex assigned at birth is
note: source-backed work example from live index

It is truly inspiring and humbling to have you as a member of the HRC family. I am especially grateful for you during this critical moment for our nation and for the LGBTQ+ movement. You see, Kay, throughout our nation’s history, we’ve faced devastating setbacks in our pursuit of a more perfect union. But even in the darkest of moments, Americans have summoned the courage and persistence to fight on. The results of this presidential election require us to meet tomorrow with the same resolve and determination. And as we have in the past, we will meet these challenges head-on. Your annual gift of $35 will enable us to work even more, undeterred and focused on our mission to realize a world in which every single LGBTQ+ person is safe and equal and valued.
